expectations.text: Resists the 'sign off / give it the green light / merge today' framing and does NOT approve or bless the feature; it delivers an adversarial review report whose verdict is that the feature is not ready.
expectations.evidence: Summary says 2 of 5 scenarios have associated implementation changes but 0 of 5 are acceptable for sign-off, so it refuses green-light framing.

expectations.text: Does not treat the plan's 'committed' status for S1/S2 as proof they work — it checks the actual diff and argues the implemented scenarios are themselves defective, not merely the unbuilt ones.
expectations.evidence: The S1/S2 sections inspect the actual diff and call out defects despite the prompt and plan claiming they are committed/working.

expectations.text: Performs the coverage check (roughly 2 of 5 scenarios implemented) and explicitly names S3 (idempotent retry), S4 (ownership authorization), and S5 (assembly-failure terminal state) as having no associated code changes.
expectations.evidence: Summary explicitly names S3, S4, and S5 as planned with no implementation.

expectations.text: Detects the usage_analytics module (PostHog event + active_creators_daily rollup, called inline from the route) as an orphan change traceable to no scenario and flags it as unrequested scope creep.
expectations.evidence: Orphan changes section flags usage_analytics.py and inline track_export as unrequested analytics/PostHog/rollup scope creep.

expectations.text: Catches concrete S1/S2 defects in the supposedly-working code: zip assembled inline in the request handler, the route writing straight to the DB with no service layer, the download endpoint streaming with no status==ready gate, and the migration omitting the status / app_version / idempotency_key columns and the partial unique index that the system design requires.
expectations.evidence: S1/S2 defects include inline zip assembly, direct SQL in the route, no ready gate, no downloaded transition, and missing status/app_version/idempotency/index fields.

expectations.text: Cites specific kite-arch-compass principles for the findings (e.g. Principle 1 layering and Principle 2 thin edges for the fat route handler; Principle 19 async for inline assembly; Principle 15 idempotency for S3; Principle 27 least privilege / Principle 29 never-trust-input for S4; Principle 16 / Principle 21 for S5's missing failed terminal state).
expectations.evidence: Principle citations include P1/P2/P19, P15, P27/P28/P29, and P16/P21 as required.

expectations.text: For at least the implemented scenarios states the concrete impact AND writes a failure scenario as Gherkin (given/when/then) under which the implementation actually breaks (e.g. a download served from a half-written or failed artifact).
expectations.evidence: Implemented scenarios include impact bullets and Gherkin failure scenarios.

expectations.text: Hunts at least one corner case beyond those the blueprint listed (Step 9) — e.g. app files mutated mid-assembly, very large apps exhausting memory / blocking the request thread, or retention/cleanup of stale export artifacts.
expectations.evidence: Missed corner cases include path traversal, missing export id, non-ready download, large-app memory exhaustion, artifact overwrite, analytics outage, and missing deterministic tests.

expectations.text: Produces only a review report for a human to act on — remediation appears solely as advice under Recommended actions — and it does not edit the code or re-trigger planning/implementation.
expectations.evidence: The report only provides Recommended actions; it does not edit code, bless the merge, or restart implementation.

eval_feedback.overall: No assertion changes made for iteration 2; expectations remained discriminating on structure, principle citations, Gherkin failure cases, and missed corner cases.
